name: Xinference
tagline: Serve LLM, embedding, and multimodal models behind one OpenAI-compatible API
metaDescription: Xinference deploys language, embedding, and multimodal models through one OpenAI-compatible API, with vLLM, llama.cpp, and Transformers backends.
overview: Xinference (Xorbits Inference) is an open-source platform for serving language, speech, embedding, and multimodal models. You launch a built-in or custom model with a single command, and it exposes that model over an OpenAI-compatible REST API, plus RPC, a CLI, and a web UI.

It is aimed at developers and data teams who want to run open models on their own hardware instead of calling a hosted API. Under the hood it can use different inference engines, including vLLM, llama.cpp, and Hugging Face Transformers, and it can spread work across GPUs and CPUs on one machine or several.

As a model-serving tool, it sits between your models and your application code. Because the API matches the OpenAI format, existing clients and libraries such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Dify can talk to it with little change.
features: OpenAI-compatible RESTful API, including a Function Calling API, plus RPC, CLI, and a web UI for managing models
Multiple inference backends: vLLM, llama.cpp (via xllamacpp), Transformers, SGLang, and MLX
Built-in catalog of open models covering chat, embeddings, speech, and multimodal tasks, each launchable with one command
Runs on heterogeneous hardware, using GPUs and CPUs, with CPU and Metal support
Distributed deployment that spreads model inference across multiple devices or machines
Integrations with LangChain, LlamaIndex, Dify, FastGPT, RAGFlow, and Chatbox

gettingStarted.intro: Install Xinference, start a local server, then launch a model and call it from the Python client.

steps.heading: Install Xinference
steps.body: Install the full package with all backends. You can also install a single backend extra such as xinference[vllm], xinference[llama_cpp], or xinference[transformers].

steps.heading: Start the local server
steps.body: Run the local server, which serves the API and web UI on the chosen host and port.

steps.heading: Launch a model
steps.body: Launch a built-in model from the CLI, choosing the inference engine, name, size, and format.

steps.heading: Call the model from Python
steps.body: Connect with the RESTful client, get the running model, and send a chat message.

useCases: Self-host open chat models behind an OpenAI-compatible endpoint so existing OpenAI client code keeps working
Serve embedding models for a RAG pipeline alongside chat and reranking models from one platform
Run inference across several GPUs or machines for larger models or higher throughput
Connect a serving backend to tools like Dify, LangChain, or LlamaIndex without writing a custom API layer
